response.justification: Strong engineer with proven ability to work on complex, large-scale systems and current developer tooling experience. However, primary skills have shifted toward TypeScript/web over the past 5 years, with no evident expertise in Java/Kotlin, Spring ecosystem, or IDE internals—all core requirements. Could potentially ramp up on JVM expertise given his polyglot background, but would need to catch up on enterprise frameworks.
strengths: Demonstrated expertise building complex, large-scale distributed systems (Sociomantic microservices handling millions of requests, real-time data processing)
Current work on developer tooling infrastructure (MCP frameworks, code generators) shows relevant architectural thinking for IDE domains
Strong software craftsmanship—organized refactoring dojos, created testing frameworks (Fluent Asserts, Trial), mentored teams, emphasis on TDD
concerns: No demonstrated expertise in Java/Kotlin or Spring ecosystem, which are primary job requirements
Career trajectory over past 5 years is primarily TypeScript/Node.js, not JVM-focused development
Limited recent IDE architecture experience—National Instruments role was 10+ years ago with C/C++, not JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: Spring and enterprise framework knowledge gap
Current developer tooling and architecture experience
